view

class vmol.view.VMolecule(atoms: Atoms, color_scheme: dict = array([[1., 0., 0.], [1., 1., 1.], [0.851, 1., 1.], [0.8, 0.502, 1.], [0.761, 1., 0.], [1., 0.71, 0.71], [0.565, 0.565, 0.565], [0.188, 0.4], [0.8, 0., 0.349], [0.82, 0., 0.31], [0.851, 0., 0.271], [0.878, 0., 0.22], [0.902, 0., 0.18], [0.922, 0., 0.149]]), show_axis: bool = False, alignment: list | None = None, frame=0, radius=0.2, **kwargs)[source]

Tool to visualize molecules, equivalent to nglview or vmd

add_angle(atom1index: int, atom2index: int, atom3index: int, color: list | None = None, n: int = 20, factor: float = 0.7) → dict[source]

Add an angle to between three atoms: atom1, atom2 and atom3 - with the vertex in the atom2

Parameters:

  • atom1index (int) – Indexes of the three atoms that defines the angle.

  • atom3index (atom2index and) – Indexes of the three atoms that defines the angle.

  • color (color list. Default all gray([0.5, 0.5, 0.5])) – RGB triplet.

  • n (int. Default 10) – number of intermedia points to add in the arc of the angle.

  • factor – factor of the minimim bond lenght to define the size of the arc.

Return type:

(vmol.tools.dofs.VisualAngle) Just created angle.

add_atoms(atoms: Atoms | None = None, color_scheme: dict = array([[1., 0., 0.], [1., 1., 1.], [0.851, 1., 1.], [0.8, 0.502, 1.], [0.761, 1., 0.], [1., 0.71, 0.71], [0.565, 0.565, 0.565], [0.188, [0.122, 0.941, 0.122], [0.502, 0.82, 0.89], [0.561, 0.251, 0.831], [0.239, 1., 0.], [0.902, 0.902, 0.902], [0.749, 0.761, 0.78], [0.651, 0.651, 0.671], [0.541, 0.6, 0.78], [0.612, 0.478, 0.78], [0.039, 0.49, 0.549], [0., 0.412, 0.522], [0.753, 0.753, 0.753], [1., 0.851, 0.561], [0.651, 0.459, 0.451], [0.4, 0.502, 0.502], [0.62, 0.388, 0.71], [0.831, 0.478, 0.], [0.58, 0., 0.58], [0.259, 0.62, 0.69], [0.341, 0.09, 0.561], [0., 0.788, 0.], [0.439, 0.831, 1.], [1., 1., 0.78], [0.851, 1., 0.78], [0.78, 1., 0.78], [0.639, 1., 0.78], [0.561, 1., 0.78], [0.38, 1., 0.78], [0.271, 1., 0.78], [0.188, 1., 0.78], [0.122, 1., 0.78], [0., 1., 0.612], [0., 0.902, 0.459], [0., 0.831, 0.322], [0., 0.749, 0.22], [0., 0.671, 0.141], [0.302, 0.761, 1.], [0.302, 0.651, 1.], [0.129, 0.58, 0.839], [0.149, 0.49, 0.671], [0.149, 0.4, 0.588], [0.09, 0.329, 0.529], [0.816, 0.816, 0.878], [1., 0.82, 0.137], [0.722, 0.722, 0.816], [0.651, 0.329, 0.302], [0.341, 0.349, 0.38], [0.62, 0.31, 0.71], [0.671, 0.361, 0.], [0.459, 0.31, 0.271], [0.259, 0.51, 0.588], [0.259, 0., 0.4], [0., 0.49, 0.], [0.439, 0.671, 0.98], [0., 0.729, 1.], [0., 0.631, 1.], [0., 0.561, 1.], [0., 0.502, 1.], [0., 0.42, 1.], [0.329, 0.361, 0.949], [0.471, 0.361, 0.89], [0.541, 0.31, 0.89], [0.631, 0.212, 0.831], [0.702, 0.122, 0.831], [0.702, 0.122, 0.729], [0.702, 0.051, 0.651], [0.741, 0.051, 0.529], [0.78, 0., 0.4], [0.8, 0., 0.349], [0.82, 0., 0.31], [0.851, 0., 0.271], [0.878, 0., 0.22], [0.902, 0., 0.18], [0.922, 0., 0.149]]), radius: float = 0.2) → list[source]

Add vpython spheres that are representations of atoms.

Parameters:

  • atoms (ase.Atoms) – set of atoms to be rendered.

  • color_scheme (dict) – color scheme of the atoms. Default = jml_colors

  • radius (float) – set the size of the atoms. Default = 0.2

Return type:

(list) set of created vpython spheres, namely, atoms in the scene.

add_bond(atom1index: int, atom2index: int, color: vector | None = None, radius: float = 0.1) → cylinder[source]

Add a bond between two atoms: atom1 and atom2

Parameters:

  • atom2index) (atom1index (and) – Indexes of the atoms to be connected according with g09 convention.

  • color (list. Default gray([0.5, 0.5, 0.5])) – RGB triplet.

  • radius (float. Default 0.1) – Radius of the bond.

Return type:

(vpython.cylinder) representation of the bond.

add_bonds(atoms1indexes: list, atoms2indexes: list, colors: list | None = None, radii: float = 0.07) → dict[source]

Add a bond between each pair of atoms corresponding to two lists of atoms: atoms1 and atoms.

Parameters:

  • atom2index) (atom1index (and) – Indexes of the atoms to be connected according with g09 convention.

  • colors (list of color lists. Default all gray([0.5, 0.5, 0.5])) – RGB triplets for each of the bonds. It can be one a triplet in case of just one color in all bonds.

  • radius (float or list of floats. Default 0.1) – radius of each bond.

Returns:

  • (dict) all the DOFs in the system. keys -> dof names,

  • values -> vpython.objects

add_dihedral(atom1index: int, atom2index: int, atom3index: int, atom4index: int, color: list | None = None, n: int = 20, factor: float = 0.7) → VisualAngle[source]

Add a dihedral angle between four atoms: atom1, atom2, atom3 and atom4 - with the vertex in the midle of the atom 2 and 3

Parameters:

  • atom1index (int) – Indexes of the three atoms that defines the angle.

  • atom2index (int) – Indexes of the three atoms that defines the angle.

  • atom4index (atom3index and) – Indexes of the three atoms that defines the angle.

  • color (color list) – RGB triplet. Default all gray([0.5, 0.5, 0.5])

  • n (int) – number of intermedia points to add in the arc of the angle. Default 20

  • factor (float) – factor of the minimim bond lenght to define the size of the arc.

Return type:

(vmol.tools.dofs.VisualAngle) Just created dihedral angle.

add_dof(dof: list, **kwargs)[source]

Add the degree of freedom to the scene

Parameters:

  • dof (list) – label of the degree of freedom according with g09 convention.

  • **kwargs (arguments corresponding with the DOF you want to add.) –

Returns:

  • Object used to represent the DOF. For further detail, check add_<dof>,

  • where <dof> could be bond, angle or dihedral.

  • Usage

  • =======

  • VMolecule.add_dof(dof), where – dof=(1, 2) means a bond between atoms 1 and 2 dof=(1, 2, 3) means an angle between atoms 1, 2 and 3 dof=(1, 2, 3, 4) means a dihedral angle between atoms 1, 2, 3 and 4

download_image(name)[source]

Save displayed scene in a png file.

Parameters:

  • name (str) – name of the image file.

  • Note (So far, I couldn't avoid to render the saving-file interface that) –

  • promps –

  • render (even when a name is given. This makes annoyng to) –

  • animation. (n-images for an) –

hide_all_angles()[source]

Hide all the angles in the scene

Returns:

  • (dict) all the DOFs in the system. keys -> dof names,

  • values -> vpython.objects

hide_all_bonds()[source]

Hide all bonds

Returns:

  • (dict) all hidden DOFs in the system. keys -> dof names,

  • values -> vpython.objects

hide_all_dihedrals()[source]

Hide all the dihedrals in the scene

Returns:

  • (dict) all the DOFs in the system. keys -> dof names,

  • values -> vpython.objects

hide_angle(atom1index: int, atom2index: int, atom3index: int) → dict[source]

Hide one angle between three atoms: atoms1, atoms2 and atom3.

Parameters:

atom3index (atom1index atom2index and) – Indexes of the atoms that are connected. This bond will be hidden.

Returns:

  • (dict) all the DOFs in the system. keys -> dof names,

  • values -> vpython.objects

hide_atom(atomindex: int) → sphere[source]

Hide one atom in the scene.

Parameters:

atomindex (int) – Index of the atom to be hidden.

Return type:

(vpython.sphere) hidden vpython atom

hide_bond(atom1index: int, atom2index: int) → dict[source]

Hide one bond between two atoms: atoms1 and atoms2.

Parameters:

atom2index) (atom1index (and) – Indexes of the atoms that are connected. This bond will be hidden.

Returns:

  • (dict) all the DOFs in the system. keys -> dof names,

  • values -> vpython.objects

hide_bonds(atoms1indexes: int | None = None, atoms2indexes: int | None = None) → dict[source]

Hide several bonds in the plot between two list of atoms: atoms1 and atoms2.

Parameters:

atom2index) (atom1index (and) – Indexes of the atoms that are connected. If None atom index is defined, all the bonds are hidden. If only one atom index is defined, all the bonds with that atom will hidden.

Returns:

  • (dict) all hidden DOFs in the system. keys -> dof names,

  • values -> vpython.objects

  • Note (if atoms2 is None, all bonds with atoms1 will me hidden.)

  • If atoms1 and atoms2 are None, all bonds in the structure are

  • hidden.

hide_dihedral(atom1index: int, atom2index: int, atom3index: int, atom4index: int)[source]

Hide one dihedral angle defined by three atoms: atoms1, atoms2 and atom3.

Parameters:

atom3index (atom1index atom2index and) – Indexes of the atoms that are connected. This bond will be hidden.

Returns:

  • (dict) all the DOFs in the system. keys -> dof names,

  • values -> vpython.objects

setting_canvas(**kwargs) → canvas[source]

Setting vpython scene.

**kwargs: vpython.canvas attibutes

show_atom(atomindex: int) → sphere[source]

Show one atom in the scene.

Parameters:

atomindex (int) – Index of the atom to be shown.

Return type:

(vpython.sphere) hidden vpython atom

show_axis(show: bool = True, size: float = 1.0, center: ~vpython.cyvector.vector = <0, 0, 0>) → list[source]

show xyz axis

Parameters:

  • show (bool) – True to show it. Default = True

  • size (float) – thickness of the arrows

  • center (vector or array) – position of the origin

Return type:

(list) list with the xyz vpytho arrows

update_atom(index: int, **kwargs) → sphere[source]

Update atom properties

Parameters:

  • index (int) – index of the atom to be modified.

  • **kwargs (arguments to be modified) –

Return type:

(vpython.sphere) sphere representing the atom

update_obj(obj, **kwargs)[source]

Changes attributes of an object. Specially useful to update positions, size and colors in vpython scene.

Parameters:

  • obj (obj) – object to be modified.

  • **kwargs (attributes and values to be modified.) –

Returns:

  • (obj) Modified Object

  • Usage

  • =====

  • VMolecule.update_obj(vpython.sphere, radius=10)

Previous
